scenarios.description: setScores assigns a single-element array (length=1) and computes height=0 (branch: scores length power-of-two)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance created via default constructor; initial scores and height saved for comparison
scenarios.expectedResult: scores array is replaced by [5]; getHeight() returns 0
pseudocode: GIVEN: a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance with initial scores and height
THEN: assert Arrays.equals(instance.getScores(), new int[]{5}) and instance.getHeight() == 0

scenarios.description: setScores assigns a 4-element array (length=4) and computes height=2 (branch: scores length power-of-two)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance created; initial state recorded
scenarios.expectedResult: scores array is replaced by [1,2,3,4]; getHeight() returns 2
pseudocode: THEN: assert Arrays.equals(instance.getScores(), new int[]{1,2,3,4}) and instance.getHeight() == 2

scenarios.description: setScores assigns an 8-element array (length=8) and computes height=3 (branch: scores length power-of-two)
scenarios.expectedResult: scores array is replaced by [10,20,30,40,50,60,70,80]; getHeight() returns 3
pseudocode: THEN: assert Arrays.equals(instance.getScores(), new int[]{10,20,30,40,50,60,70,80}) and instance.getHeight() == 3

scenarios.description: setScores rejects a 3-element array (length=3 not power-of-two) leaving scores and height unchanged (branch: scores length not power-of-two)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance created; originalScores = instance.getScores(); originalHeight = instance.getHeight();
scenarios.expectedResult: instance.getScores() equals originalScores and instance.getHeight() equals originalHeight; printed output contains "The number of scores must be a power of 2."
pseudocode: GIVEN: a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance with saved originalScores and originalHeight
THEN: assert Arrays.equals(instance.getScores(), originalScores) and instance.getHeight() == originalHeight and console output contains "The number of scores must be a power of 2."
